summary: High‑energy 1984 Vegas show with blazing fast Eyes and stellar China Cat→Rider jam; soundboard capture is generally clean but has noticeable cracks and vocal flubs.
review: The performance bursts with tight jams – the rapid "Eyes" out of Space, a thunderous "Might As Well", and an all‑time favorite China Cat→Rider sequence dominate the set.  While the soundboard source is mostly crisp, listeners note crackle around "Bertha", a vocal flub on "Brown Eyed Girl" and some abrupt cuts in the middle of songs.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (≈70 %) give 4‑5 stars and praise the musical flow; a minority cite lyrical slip‑ups and recording glitches that pull the average down.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clean and clear with good instrument separation.  Notable issues: crackle/"mix adjustments" around "Bertha", a vocal delay on the early "Brown Eyed Girl" verse, occasional tuning pauses and abrupt cuts in "Saint of Circumstance" and "Black Peter".

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds with fast‑paced jams, but tension between Jerry and Bob shows up in the "Music Never Stopped" ending.  The second set erupts into a smoking run – rapid Eyes, an extended Black Peter/Truckin' tease, then the beloved China Cat→Rider jam before winding down with soulful ballads.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fast‑paced "Eyes" and a thunderous Mighty Swell; noted for plugging into Aladdin's amp.  Vocal flubs on early songs and a brief musical “argument” with Bob over song order.
band_member_comments.Phil: "ON" – Phil’s bass is highlighted as especially solid; several reviewers call his playing "on" throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: His slide fills on Black Peter receive praise, but he forgets lyrics in "Music Never Stopped" and clashes with Jerry over set transitions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (Keith) gets a rave for an all‑time best solo on "All Over Now" and his chimey presence shines during the China Cat→Rider jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Long tuning pauses before the “Jerry‑less” jam are noted; drum kit is described as “rising” and adding height to the improvisations.
